g.text: Cache Line Heat Map — epoll Dispatch Hot Path (reactor.c)
PHASE 2b — void* udata pointer chase: http_conn_t*
HOT PATH COST SUMMARY — 100K events/sec steady state
12B per entry | 1024 entries = 12 288 B = 192 cache lines
COST: L1 HIT — kernel copy_to_user is sequential prefetch-friendly
40 B per fd_handler_t | 65536 slots = 2.56 MB >> L2 (256 KB)
COST: RANDOM fd values = random cache miss per event = ~100ns each
Total: 40 B — does NOT fit in single 64-B cache line
HOT fields: callback+udata = 16 B at offset 0x10-0x1F
Total: ~26 KB | scattered across ~406 cache lines
COST: 2nd pointer chase after handlers[fd].udata — DRAM if cold
TLB flush | CR3 switch | ~200 cycles (~67 ns)
TOTAL per read(): ~1 000 ns (dominates if data < 1 KB)
At 100 K events/sec: 100 000 x 1000 ns = 100 ms/sec of syscall overhead
sk_buff[0] | 1448 B (MSS) | page cache | ~40 ns L3
sk_buff[1] | 1448 B (MSS) | page cache | ~40 ns L3
sk_buff[2] | partial | page cache | ~40 ns L3
copy_to_user copies sk_buff chain → conn->read_buf sequentially
Sequential pages → hardware prefetcher fires → stays in L3
timer_heap[MAX_TIMERS] int array — root always hot
heap[n] | deep nodes | rarely touched | DRAM
int array: 4 B per entry | 65536 entries = 256 KB = L3
COST: sift_up/down touches O(log n)=13 nodes per insert/cancel
32 B fits in half a cache line (64 B) — 2 entries per line
Sift touches 13 entries x 32 B = 416 B = ~7 cache lines
PROBLEM: handlers[] 2.56 MB >> L2 | RANDOM fd = cache miss per event
FIX A: Sort events[] by fd after epoll_wait → spatial locality
FIX B: Pack hot fields (cb+udata) in first 16 B of fd_handler_t
PROBLEM: http_conn_t 26 KB scattered | cold ptr chase per event
FIX C: Hot/cold split: struct hot_fields {fd,state,cb,udata} 32 B
FIX D: Huge pages (mmap MAP_HUGETLB) for handlers[] → TLB hits
PROBLEM: conn->read_buf 16 KB cold | DRAM miss after idle period
PROBLEM: 100 K events/sec x 100 ns miss = 10 ms/sec in cache stalls
FIX F: Limit active connections to L3-fitting set (~8192 at 26 KB)
Per-event cost breakdown (one EPOLLIN on one fd)
events[i] load | L1 HIT | ~1 ns | sequential array
handlers[fd] load | DRAM MISS | ~100 ns | random fd scatter
h->udata ptr chase | DRAM MISS | ~100 ns | heap-alloc conn
conn->read_buf access | DRAM MISS | ~100 ns | 16 KB cold buffer
read() syscall | SYSCALL | ~1000 ns| user-kernel switch
parse (sequential buf) | L1 HIT | ~10 ns | just-filled buffer
write() syscall | SYSCALL | ~1000 ns| kernel copy out
TOTAL dominant cost: 2x SYSCALL (~2 us) + 3x DRAM (~300 ns)
At 100K events/sec SYSCALL overhead = 200ms/sec CPU (20% of 1 core)
